projects.latheacq.overview.title: site.hopen
Overview
site.hclose
projects.latheacq.overview.1: One weekend, whilst on the prowl for useful discarded materials, I happened across what appeared to be part of a lathe. It was in
an advanced state of decrepitude and appeared to be missing both the tool-rest and tailstock. The latter, however, was eventually
projects.latheacq.overview.2: I had my wife stand guard over the machine whilst I negotiated a price and paid for it. I then carried it several hundred metres
back to the car, only to discover later that it weighed just over 85kg without the tailstock attached.
projects.latheacq.overview.3: At the time, it was unclear whether the machine would be restored or cannibalised for parts and scrap. I'd already been attempting
to build a metal lathe from first principles, but progress was slow and difficult. Perhaps this machine could be restored to working
condition — or perhaps it could serve as the foundation for building something else.
projects.latheacq.overview.4: Once I examined it properly, however, it seemed a shame simply to scrap it. The old machine was a mess, but I was up for the challenge.
projects.latheacq.gallery.title: I Found a Lathe!
